summary: I re-traced the iteration-3 technical design against all 34 business requirements and emitted a **pass** verdict. The two gaps flagged in iteration 2 are now genuinely closed: the major F1 (a completed "Repeat from the Beginning" restart must show its fresh, replaced results — including the regenerated `business_requirements`, Define's headline deliverable that renders nowhere in the UI today) is resolved by the new `define_business_requirements` helper (§6.2) and the inline "Requirements" block placed before the paused menu (§6.3); the minor F2 (Ask Human unreachable when a paused Define has no open questions yet) is resolved by widening the answer block's condition to `questions.present? || phase.paused?` with an empty-state notes field (§2.9, §6.3). All 34 requirements — including the newly-added R12 — map to concrete, cited design elements, the existing-behavior requirements (R32–R34) hold via the strictly-additive approach, and there is no meaningful gold-plating (only a trivial unused timestamp column and unpersisted `user:` params, both self-acknowledged as future-audit hooks). Findings are empty and the verdict is written to `verdict.json`.
